stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the Meta-Llama-3-8B model, a significant large language model.
It is featured on Hugging Face Benchmarks, indicating community interest and performance tracking.
The associated pipeline is 'text-generation,' confirming its primary application in generative AI.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model.
It is categorized under text-generation pipelines, indicating its primary function in language modeling.
The associated tags (transformers, safetensors, llama) confirm its relevance to the modern NLP/LLM ecosystem.

stories.title: History Anchors: How Prior Behavior Steers LLM Decisions Toward Unsafe Actions
stories.author: Alberto G. Rodríguez Salgado
bullet_points: Research investigates how prior harmful actions in a sequence of tool calls influence the subsequent decisions of frontier LLM agents.
The study introduces 'HistoryAnchor-100,' a benchmark testing LLMs across ten high-stakes domains with forced harmful preceding steps.
The findings address a critical safety concern: whether models will continue a harmful trajectory established by past behavior.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text-generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for enhanced performance.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware, optimizing AI workloads on Macs.
The feature is currently available in a preview state, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs directly on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI capabilities for coding assistance.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to streamline the setup and execution of coding tools.
It allows users to run tools like Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex using local or cloud models.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is partnering with OpenAI and ROOST to integrate the gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models.
These models are designed for safety classification tasks and are available in 20B and 120B sizes.
The gpt-oss-safeguard models are permissively licensed under the Apache 2.0 license, promoting open use.

stories.title: Android’s latest AI feature predicts what you’ll do next
stories.author: Jess Weatherbed
bullet_points: Google is deploying a new AI feature on Android called 'contextual suggestions'.
The feature uses location and user habits to predict and recommend the user's next action.
Examples include automatically suggesting a user's usual music playlist upon arriving at a specific location.

stories.title: Microsoft’s Edge Copilot update uses AI to pull information from across your tabs
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Microsoft Edge Copilot is being updated to leverage AI across all open browser tabs.
Users can now ask Copilot questions, compare items, and summarize content from their active tabs.
This integration represents a significant step in making AI assistants context-aware within the browsing experience.

stories.title: WARDEN: Endangered Indigenous Language Transcription and Translation with 6 Hours of Training Data
stories.author: Liang Zheng
bullet_points: Introduces WARDEN, an early language model designed for transcription and translation.
Addresses the critical challenge of low-resource languages by operating with only 6 hours of annotated audio data.
Demonstrates a viable approach to NLP tasks for endangered indigenous languages where large datasets are unavailable.

stories.title: EVA-Bench: A New End-to-end Framework for Evaluating Voice Agents
stories.author: Srinivas Sunkara
bullet_points: EVA-Bench is a new end-to-end framework designed to evaluate voice agents, which are AI systems that handle spoken conversations for tasks.
It specifically addresses two major gaps in current evaluation: generating realistic simulated conversations and measuring quality across voice-specific failure modes.
The framework orchestrates bot-to-bot interactions to provide a comprehensive assessment of voice agent performance in enterprise applications.

stories.title: Negation Neglect: When models fail to learn negations in training
stories.author: Owain Evans
bullet_points: Introduces 'Negation Neglect,' a failure mode where fine-tuning LLMs on documents flagging a claim as false causes the model to believe the claim is true.
The issue persists even when the model can correctly identify the claim as false when presented in a contextual setting.
This highlights a critical vulnerability in LLM training and fine-tuning processes related to handling negation and factual contradiction.

stories.title: Neurosymbolic Auditing of Natural-Language Software Requirements
stories.author: William Eiers
bullet_points: Proposes a neurosymbolic approach to audit ambiguous and inconsistent natural-language software requirements.
Integrates Large Language Models (LLMs) with SMT solvers to translate requirements into formal logic.
Detects specification flaws like ambiguity and inconsistency by analyzing stochastic variations in formalization.

stories.title: WARDEN: Endangered Indigenous Language Transcription and Translation with 6 Hours of Training Data
stories.author: Liang Zheng
bullet_points: Introduction of WARDEN, an early language model designed for transcription and translation.
The system addresses the critical challenge of low-resource language processing, specifically for the endangered Wardaman language.
WARDEN demonstrates viability with extremely limited data, using only 6 hours of annotated audio for training.

stories.title: Negation Neglect: When models fail to learn negations in training
stories.author: Owain Evans
bullet_points: Introduces 'Negation Neglect,' a failure mode where LLMs incorrectly learn claims as true despite being trained on documents explicitly flagging them as false.
The issue manifests when finetuning LLMs on documents that contain a claim alongside repeated warnings that the claim is untrue.
This failure persists even when the models are able to correctly identify the claim as false when presented in a standard contextual setting.

stories.title: RTLC -- Research, Teach-to-Learn, Critique: A three-stage prompting paradigm inspired by the Feynman Learning Technique that lifts LLM-as-judge accuracy on JudgeBench with no fine-tuning
stories.author: Andrea Morandi
bullet_points: Introduces RTLC, a novel three-stage prompting paradigm (Research, Teach-to-Learn, Critique) to improve LLM-as-judge performance.
Addresses the current weakness of LLM-as-judge systems on objective-correctness tasks on benchmarks like JudgeBench.
Achieves improved judging accuracy without requiring any fine-tuning, external tools, or retrieval mechanisms.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/AI tags like 'transformers' and 'pytorch'.
The pipeline indicates the model is being used for 'text-generation'.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now offers experimental image generation capabilities.
The feature is currently available for local use on macOS.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage Claude Code capabilities within Ollama.
It bridges the gap between open-source local models and proprietary APIs like Anthropic's.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a user's directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: AT&T, T-Mobile, and Verizon team up to eliminate coverage dead zones
stories.author: Jess Weatherbed
bullet_points: AT&T, T-Mobile, and Verizon have formed a joint venture to combat wireless dead zones across the US.
The partnership involves the three carriers pooling their ground-based spectrum resources.
The primary goal is to significantly improve wireless coverage, particularly in rural areas.

stories.title: Windows Update will soon automatically roll back faulty drivers
stories.author: Tom Warren
bullet_points: Microsoft is enhancing Windows Update to improve the user experience.
A new feature will automatically roll back problematic drivers installed via Windows Update.
Users will soon have the ability to pause Windows Updates indefinitely.

stories.title: Desperate Trump taps "Tim Apple," Jensen Huang, Elon Musk to attend Xi summit
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: The story suggests a high-level diplomatic effort involving key tech leaders (Tim Apple, Jensen Huang, Elon Musk) to influence US policy.
The potential attendance at a Xi summit indicates a possible pivot by Trump regarding semiconductor tariffs and Taiwan policy.
The relevance lies in the intersection of geopolitics, US trade policy, and the critical semiconductor industry.

stories.title: AI invades Princeton, where 30% of students cheat—but peers won't snitch
stories.author: Nate Anderson
bullet_points: AI tools are challenging traditional academic integrity systems at Princeton.
A significant portion (30%) of students are reportedly using AI to cheat.
Peer culture is contributing to the problem, as students are reluctant to report cheating.

stories.title: A new US military wargame series began by simulating a nuclear weapon in orbit
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: A new US military wargame series is simulating the impact of a nuclear weapon in orbit.
Officials estimate a nuclear detonation could render parts of low-Earth orbit unusable for up to a year.
The simulation highlights the potential strategic vulnerability of orbital assets to nuclear conflict.

stories.title: Improving Reproducibility in Evaluation through Multi-Level Annotator Modeling
stories.author: Christopher M. Homan
bullet_points: The paper addresses the reproducibility crisis in AI evaluation, particularly for generative models like LLMs.
It focuses on the inherent unreliability and bias introduced by human raters in model assessment.
The proposed solution involves using Multi-Level Annotator Modeling to improve the consistency of evaluations.

stories.title: EVA-Bench: A New End-to-end Framework for Evaluating Voice Agents
stories.author: Srinivas Sunkara
bullet_points: Introduces EVA-Bench, a new end-to-end framework for evaluating voice agents.
Addresses two core evaluation gaps: generating realistic simulated conversations and measuring voice-specific failure modes.
Relevant as voice agents are increasingly deployed in enterprise applications.

stories.title: Good Agentic Friends Do Not Just Give Verbal Advice: They Can Update Your Weights
stories.author: Yuzhang Shang
bullet_points: Proposes an alternative communication interface for multi-agent LLM systems to move beyond simple natural-language message exchange.
Addresses the computational inefficiencies (token cost, prefill overhead, KV-cache memory) inherent in current token-based LLM collaboration.
Suggests compiling a sender's hidden states into a transient representation instead of serializing them into text tokens.

stories.title: An LLM-Based System for Argument Reconstruction
stories.author: Douglas Aldred
bullet_points: Introduces an end-to-end LLM-based system for argument reconstruction.
The system converts natural language text into abstract argument graphs.
It uses a multi-stage pipeline to identify components and uncover logical relations.

stories.title: Where Does Reasoning Break? Step-Level Hallucination Detection via Hidden-State Transport Geometry
stories.author: Ali Baheri
bullet_points: The research addresses the limitation of existing LLM hallucination detectors, which operate only at the full output (trace) level.
The paper proposes framing hallucination as a property of the hidden-state trajectory during a single forward pass.
It introduces a method to detect errors by analyzing the geometry of the hidden-state transitions, distinguishing coherent reasoning from localized excursions.

stories.title: Senses Wide Shut: A Representation-Action Gap in Omnimodal LLMs
stories.author: Ziwei Liu
bullet_points: The paper investigates the 'Representation-Action Gap' in multimodal LLMs when textual premises contradict sensory input (video/audio).
It challenges the assumption that current omnimodal models are fully perception-grounded by testing this conflict detection.
The authors introduce IMAVB, a new 500-clip benchmark derived from long-form movies to test this grounding issue.

stories.title: Children's English Reading Story Generation via Supervised Fine-Tuning of Compact LLMs with Controllable Difficulty and Safety
stories.author: Walter L. Leite
bullet_points: Addresses the dual challenges of LLM-generated children's stories being too difficult and being computationally expensive.
Proposes fine-tuning smaller, compact 8B-parameter LLMs using an expert-designed curriculum for better educational application.
Focuses on achieving controllable difficulty and safety in story generation, leveraging existing high-quality models (GPT-4o, Llama 3.3 70B) for data.

stories.title: Old PC vs New AI: Can a 2015 Desktop Actually Run Gemma 4? (2B vs 4B Benchmark)
stories.author: gramli
bullet_points: The article explores the feasibility of running modern AI models (like Gemma 4) on older, less powerful hardware (a 2015 desktop).
It focuses on benchmarking smaller model sizes (2B vs 4B) to determine performance viability on legacy systems.
The topic addresses the growing trend of local, on-device AI inference, even on constrained hardware.

stories.title: DeepSeek-V4: Finally, a Context Window Built for Agents
stories.author: o96a
bullet_points: The article discusses DeepSeek-V4, a new large language model.
The model is highlighted for having a context window specifically designed for AI agents.
It is positioned as being different from other long-context models that are often just benchmarked.
